steps.title: Identify the perimeter of the rectangle
steps.content: A rectangle has four edges: two lengths of $x$ feet each and two widths of $y$ feet each. Fencing along each edge means covering the entire perimeter: $x + x + y + y = 2x + 2y$ feet.

steps.title: Set the perimeter equal to the total fencing
steps.content:
Malia used 58 feet of fencing, so:
$$2x + 2y = 58$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $2x+2y=58$: The perimeter of a rectangle is twice the length plus twice the width, which equals 58. ✓
  • Choice B — $x+y=58$: This only sums one length and one width — it forgets that a rectangle has two of each. ✗
  • Choice C — $2xy=58$: This involves a product ($xy$), which gives area, not perimeter. ✗
  • Choice D — $xy=58$: $xy$ is the area formula, not the perimeter. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
